title: Apply Visual System v2 to the software pilot
goal: Compile a real software-pilot snapshot into a source-bound, narrative and quality-gated Visual System v2 board while preserving the existing renderer as an explicit compatibility path.

approval.note: Repository changes require a complete GitHub diff, head- and diff-bound self-review, green CI and exact post-merge validation. A live Miro write may target only a newly created isolated board.

acceptance.assertion: A validated software-pilot snapshot compiles deterministically into a Visual System v2 board specification and Miro DSL.
The generated board passes the v2 quality gate with at least 90 points, no blockers and explicit evidence separation.
Source authority, revisions, tests, risks and non-claims remain visible and digest-bound; missing or failed source data cannot appear current.
The existing software-pilot renderer and command remain compatible while v2 outputs are opt-in until adoption evidence is complete.

One fresh isolated Miro board is created from the generated v2 DSL and checked by remote conformance plus a bounded visual review, or the unavailable provider evidence is recorded as an explicit open gate.
Tests, CI, complete diff, diff-bound self-review, mergeability and post-merge validation pass before the task is verified.
